title: Adicea rugosa
taxonomy: Adicea rugosa belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Adicea rugosa is closely related to other species within the same family. The genus Adicea is thought to be a monotypic genus, meaning that it is represented by a single species. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of Adicea rugosa within the plant kingdom.
uses: Adicea rugosa is reportedly used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
